title: Abort/Segfault: unchecked NULL in `channelsmod__channel_id` (`_interpchannelsmodule.c:3487`)
description: `Modules/_interpchannelsmodule.c`: `channelsmod__channel_id` (`_interpchannels._channel_id`) calls `get_module_from_owned_type` -> `_get_current_module`, which can return `NULL` under OOM, then does `assert(mod == self)` and `Py_DECREF(mod)` without a NULL check. Debug builds abort on the assert; release builds segfault on `Py_DECREF(NULL)`.
notes: Build-agnostic NULL-pointer defect (not free-threading-specific). channelsmod__channel_id (L3478) does `PyObject *mod = get_module_from_owned_type(cls); assert(mod == self); Py_DECREF(mod);` at L3486-3488 with no NULL check. get_module_from_owned_type (L165) is a shim over _get_current_module (L149), whose first step PyUnicode_FromString(MODULE_NAME_STR) (L151) fails under OOM and returns NULL with an exception set. Debug builds (ft_debug_asan, jit) abort on assert(mod == self) at L3487; ft_release (NDEBUG) segfaults on Py_DECREF(NULL) at L3488 (confirmed in gdb); upstream segfaults a bit earlier inside PyImport_GetModule reached from _get_current_module L155 (same frame/site, alloc-timing-shifted). Deterministic at start=0 since the first allocation is inside _get_current_module. Fix: check `if (mod == NULL) return NULL;` before the assert/Py_DECREF (or use the infallible PyType_GetModule(cls) per the XXX at L169). The sibling shim get_module_from_type (L176) has the same latent hazard. Six vehicles across python-5 and python-7 all hit _interpchannelsmodule.c:3487.
